title: PLX-2

{{Quote|The Plex is lightweight, a nice change from other systems. But it can't take rough handling or the targeting system falls apart. Oh, you can fix it all right with regulator maintenance tools, but that takes about an hour. And there's no time for that if you're in the middle of action.|Reynol Kosk|<ref name="ISB" />}}
The '''PLX-2''' was a portable [[Missile launcher/Legends|missile launcher]] manufactured by [[Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc./Legends|Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc.]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. It was effective against [[Repulsorcraft/Legends|repulsorcraft]], as well as vehicles, atmospheric flying craft, fortifications, and other missiles. The PLX-2 was a lightweight shoulder-mounted launcher operated by a single [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]]. The launcher housed one missile in the chamber, and a second in reserve. It could fire two separate kind of missiles rounds; "dumb" rockets and [[Gravity-Activated Mode guided missile|GAM guided missiles]]. The "dumb" rockets were fired in a line-of-sight attack, but the GAM guided missiles could be programmed to track a target. The GAM guided missile supported two tracking modes, which were selectable by a firing stud on the launcher itself. In EPR mode, the missile locked onto to [[Infrared/Legends|IR]] sources such as vehicle exhaust or missile contrails. In Gravity-Activated Mode—after which the missile is named—the missile's targeting [[Computer/Legends|computer]] recorded the target's silhouette and pursued it until close enough to lock onto the [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]]-wave anomalies from the target's [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]]. A GAM guide missile in GAM mode could track a target for forty [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] before running out of fuel, although it could be confused by other targets and evasive maneuvers.<ref name="ISB" />

The PLX-2 was not very rugged, and improper use and maintenance could damage the targeting system. While a launcher with a damaged targeting system could still fire the "dumb" rockets, launching a GAM missile resulted in the launcher selecting a targeting mode without the operator being able to tell which one was chosen.<ref name="ISB" />

The firing stud on the launcher required two presses to launch a rocket; one to set the guidance system, and the one to actually fire the missile. Inexperienced troopers were known to hurry and panic in combat and only press the stud once, resulted in the missile not firing and the soldier wondering what happened.<ref name="ISB" />

A refined version of the weapon, the [[PLX-2M portable missile launcher]], was released by Merr-Sonn.<ref name="EGWT">''[[The Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]''</ref>

==History==
It first started as a [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] prototype to replace the [[PLX-1/Legends|PLX-1 portable missile launcher]] and was used by some [[Clone commando/Legends|Republic commandos]].<ref name="Hard Contact" />
